Rafael,

Thanks again for the product demo last week — the team at Elliott is excited about the throughput and the KV-cache savings. We are evaluating a 24-month prepay to lock in unit economics, but Procurement has two asks before we can move to legal:

1) Can Redwood support an annual re-opener (yearly true-up) so we can adjust the second-year commitment if our volume ramps faster/slower? We expect uneven growth across Q3–Q6.
2) We need split billing options: an 80/20 non-refundable allocation at signing (to protect Redwood) with the remainder billed annually with a true-up credit or top-up.

text:
From: Anita Singh <anita@skyward.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: High P95 for model v8q impacting production inference

Hi Kimberly / Priyom,

We started seeing a sustained P95 increase on requests routed to model v8q around 08:50 PDT this morning. Tail latency has jumped from ~420ms to ~1.2s and our user-facing timeout rate climbed as a result. This is affecting batch and single-shot flows.

Attached are a small set of traces and the live SLO dashboard (screenshot). Can you confirm whether v8q had a rollout or config change in the last 24 hours? If possible, we need either an immediate mitigation (variant fallback / rollback) or an ETA for a fix.

We are on a tight SLA window for the next two hours and would like to avoid customer-visible errors.

Thanks,
Anita Singh
Head of Platform E...

- Uptime: we proposed 99.95% for critical production endpoints (Table A). Please confirm whether Redwood can commit or provide an equivalent higher-tier offering.
- Service credits: the matrix in section 3.2 was adjusted to make credits on a sliding scale starting at 0.5% for <99.95, 2% for <99.9, and 5% for <99.0. We asked for a cap tied to monthly fees.

Also, our security team flagged maintenance windows that could occur during our business hours. We prefer mai...

Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Rerank & embeddings evaluation — next steps (Crestline Analytics)

Hi Emma — great call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned:

Recap:
- Crestline uses a hybrid retrieval stack (Elasticsearch + ANN) for product search; current reranker is TensorFlow-based on top-20 candidates.
- Key success metrics you care about: recall@10, recall@1 for conversions, retrieval latency (95th percentile), and per-query cost.
- Typical request distribution: ~70% short queries (2-4 tokens), 30% long queries (6-20 tokens).

What we propose for the competitive evaluation:
